name: Lakambini
description: Lakambini, in Filipino mythology, is a significant deity associated with purity, nourishment, and abundance. Often described as a goddess of food, particularly of the throat and stomach, she embodies the provision and sustenance crucial for life. Her name, derived from the Tagalog word "lakambini" meaning "pure woman" or "princess", reflects her association with ideal femininity and virtue. Lakambini is also sometimes viewed as a patron deity of cooks and those who prepare food, ensuring that meals are not only nourishing but also imbued with blessings and positive energy. While less prominent in some modern retellings compared to other major deities like Bathala or Mayari, Lakambini remains a vital figure in understanding the ancient Filipino reverence for food as a sacred gift. Stories depict her as a benevolent goddess who cares for the people, ensuring they have enough to eat and promoting prosperity within the community. She is invoked for good harvests, successful cooking, and a healthy appetite. Her influence highlights the importance of food not just for survival, but also as a cultural and spiritual element in Filipino society. In some accounts, she is also linked to fertility and the continuation of life, further emphasizing her role as a provider and nurturer. Lakambini represents the abundance of the earth and the blessings bestowed upon those who respect and appreciate the gifts of nature.
subtitle: Goddess of Food and Purity

keyMyths.title: Lakambini's Blessing
keyMyths.description: Lakambini blesses the first rice harvest, ensuring a plentiful yield for the entire community. Her presence brings prosperity and eliminates hunger among the people.

keyMyths.title: The Gift of Cooking
keyMyths.description: Lakambini teaches humans the art of cooking, transforming raw ingredients into nourishing meals. She shows them how to create delicious and satisfying food that sustains both body and soul.

keyMyths.title: Purity's Reward
keyMyths.description: A young woman, pure of heart, prays to Lakambini for guidance. Lakambini answers her prayers, granting her wisdom and blessing her with a life of abundance and happiness.

sources.author: Eugenio, Damiana L.
sources.description: Collection of Philippine myths and legends.
sources.text: Collection of Philippine myths and legends.

sources.author: Jocano, F. Landa
sources.work: Filipino Indigenous Ethnic Communities: Patterns, Variations, and Typologies
